Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper identifies GmIF6H1 as governing glycitein/glycitein-type isoflavonoid biosynthesis in soybean isoflavonoid metabolism.

Breeding_Application_Value.reason: The abstract/discussion state the findings offer opportunities for engineering disease-resilient soybean cultivars and show precise tuning is essential because both loss and overexpression increase susceptibility.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results show GmIF6H1 converts daidzein to 6-hydroxydaidzein, establishing the predominant route to glycitein, and also acts on formononetin and biochanin A in vitro.

Chromosome_Position.reason: The lead SNP is explicitly given as 8,259,672 bp on chromosome 11, and GmIF6H1 is the candidate in that region; exact gene coordinates are not provided.

Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement.reason: It is a cytochrome P450; microsomal assays used an NADPH regenerating system, Mn2+ and Ca2+ enhanced activity, Fe2+/Fe3+/Cu2+ inhibited activity, and P450 heme dependence is inherent and also consistent with docking to heme.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: CRISPR mutants nearly lacked glycitein-type isoflavonoids and were more susceptible to P. sojae; overexpression altered 6-hydroxydaidzein/glycitein-related products, reduced glyceollin I during infection, and also increased susceptibility.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in Results: mGWAS, hairy-root OE, yeast feeding, microsomal assay/kinetics, CRISPR, stable-isotope tracing, soybean OE, pathogen assay, and chemical complementation.

Developmental_or_Physiological_Context.reason: These contexts are explicitly described: natural population seed profiling, 2-d germinating hypocotyls, mature roots, and 3-d-old etiolated seedlings infected with P. sojae.

Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product.reason: The study directly demonstrates enzymatic conversion toward the glycitein branch and in planta labeled product formation.

Effect_Direction_on_Target_Product.reason: Loss of function strongly reduces glycitein-type isoflavonoids, so GmIF6H1 is required for their accumulation.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: GmIF6H1 forms 6-hydroxydaidzein, described as an intermediate/direct product en route to glycitein, i.e. an immediate precursor.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper explicitly redesignates GmCYP76F17 as GmIF6H1, isoflavone 6-hydroxylase 1.

Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function.reason: Yeast conversion of daidzein to 6-hydroxydaidzein, kinetic analysis, and daidzein-d6 tracing to glycitein-d5 derivatives in WT/OE but not mutant are all reported.

Evidence_for_Pathway_Position.reason: Mutants lacked glycitein-type compounds and accumulated daidzin/malonyldaidzin; OE accumulated 6-hydroxydaidzein derivatives; 6-hydroxyliquiritigenin was not detected; daidzein-d6 feeding supported pathway placement.

Favorable_Allele.reason: Hap2 (Thr248) is associated with higher glycitein/glycitein-derivative levels and higher catalytic efficiency than Hap1.

Field_Trials.reason: Seed phenotyping was performed on natural populations grown in Beijing in 2013 and Beijing/Heilongjiang/Henan in 2014; no disease-resistance field trial is reported.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Results text gives the gene as Glyma.11G108300, whereas the field states Glyma.11g108300. Case/style differs; strict accession matching is not exact in the paper.

Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method.reason: The gene was identified by mGWAS and candidate-gene screening within the lead locus, then functionally validated.

Gene_Name.reason: The paper uses GmCYP76F17 encoded by Glyma.11G108300 and states it was redesigned as GmIF6H1.

Genetic_Population.reason: The paper reports 727 accessions for profiling/GWAS, 528 for haplotypes, W82 for mutants and OE lines, and Kefeng-1 for hairy roots.

Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These factors are all explicitly included in the study: P. sojae infection, daidzein-d6 feeding, germination-stage hypocotyls, and greenhouse/multi-location seed growth.

Key_Variant_Site.reason: The paper states one SNP at +742 in the coding region causes Ala248 in Hap1 versus Thr248 in Hap2.

Key_Variant_Type.reason: The causal variation described is a SNP.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: GmIF6H1 acts in a downstream branch from daidzein toward glycitein and competes with the glyceollin branch.

Metabolite_Assay_Method.reason: LC-qToF-MS, LC-MS, and LC-QQQ-MS/MS are all explicitly described and used.

Notes_or_Other_Important_Info.reason: Each statement is reflected in the paper: prior candidate naming, renaming to GmIF6H1, higher in vitro activity on formononetin/biochanin A but absence in hypocotyls, and both LOF/OE increasing susceptibility.

Omics_Data.reason: Datasets S1-S3 and resequencing/GWAS including 53 soja accessions are stated; transcriptome profiling comparing GmIF6H1OE-1 and W82 during infection is also mentioned, though no accession is given.

Other_Phenotypic_Effects.reason: No nodulation difference was observed, and infection profiling showed altered afromosin/formononetin-type balance in OE.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: This is exactly the glycitein-type isoflavonoid branch from daidzein described in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper directly shows overexpression increases susceptibility by lowering glyceollin accumulation, implying a defense tradeoff.

Primary_Product.reason: Direct product identified in yeast and microsomes is 6-hydroxydaidzein.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper concludes daidzein is the bona fide substrate in planta and specific substrate in the key assay.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: GmIF6H1 is described as a cytochrome P450 in the CYP76/F17 lineage.

QTL_or_Locus_Name.reason: The label qMGly_11 does not appear in the provided paper text; only the Chr. 11 locus/lead SNP is described.

Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations.reason: All listed quantitative changes are reported: ~1.5-fold daidzin/malonyldaidzin increase in mutants, ~2-3-fold 6-hydroxydaidzein derivative accumulation in OE, kinetic values for Hap1/Hap2, and glyceollin I values 5.90 vs 9.36 at 2 dpi.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper clearly presents GmIF6H1 as a key determinant/branch-balancing enzyme, but does not explicitly call it rate-limiting.

Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly gives Glycine max (L.) Merr.

Target_Metabolite_Product.reason: The gene is directly linked to glycitein and glycitein-type isoflavonoids.

Target_Product_Level_or_Property.reason: The paper measures content/accumulation and discusses flux partitioning against glyceollins.

Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment.reason: Metabolites were assessed in seed, hypocotyl, root, and hairy root samples.

Variety_or_Cultivar.reason: Williams 82 and Kefeng-1 are both explicitly used.
